new_classes.label_en: Wine with Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) in containers > 2 L – other (subheading 2204 29 80)
new_classes.label_de: Wein mit geschützter geografischer Angabe (g.g.A.) in Behältnissen > 2 L – andere (Unterposition 2204 29 80)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI / g.g.A.) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres; subheading 2204 29 80 of the EU Combined Nomenclature.

coverage_explanation: The legal text identifies CN 22042980 as wine with Protected Geographical Indication (g.g.A./PGI) as defined in Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers holding more than 2 litres (subheading 2204 29 – "andere", i.e. non-sparkling / non-semi-sparkling still wine). The key distinguishing criteria are: (1) PGI status → captured via ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality restriction; (2) container volume > 2 L → captured via LargeContainer part restriction (≥ 2 L; the TBox LargeContainer is defined as ≥ 2 L which matches the > 2 L boundary for this subheading given the 2 L threshold separates 2204 21 from 2204 29); (3) still wine (not sparkling, not semi-sparkling) → captured by complement restrictions on SparklingWineQuality and SemiSparklingPressureQuality. Residual gap: Additional Note 6(a) to Chapter 22 provides a detailed legal definition of PGI for wine that cannot be fully formalised within the current TBox (no dedicated Note 6(a) individual or class is present), reducing coverage slightly. The sibling distinction from 22042979 (same PGI / container category but different sub-type or quality tier within PGI wines) is not fully axiomatised due to insufficient detail in the provided legal text for that further split.
